prompt: Defend a genuinely unpopular or unfashionable opinion that you actually hold. Not a strawman — something you'd stand by.
response: I don't believe that the most effective and equitable way to increase access to education - and therefore empower people with knowledge - is by making higher education "free" for everyone. Factors such as the individualized value that each person derives from education, the burden it places on taxpayers (some of whom may not have had the chance to attend college themselves), and the reality that "free" college often leads to a decrease in the value of a degree (because of increased surplus of graduates and diminished quality of education) are points that strongly support my opinion. Instead, I believe in finding ways to make education more accessible through scholarships, grants, and income-share agreements that align the interest of the educational institution with the student's success after graduation.
